key_insight: PEARL combines reinforcement learning with adjoint-based physics sensitivities to learn near-optimal control policies faster and more stably in differentiable dynamical systems.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical route to real-time control of high-dimensional, parametric systems with fewer environment interactions and better generalization across scenarios.
method: Uses an actor-adjoint algorithm for policy optimization over short horizons.
Employs automatic differentiation to compute gradients through the dynamics.
Approximates long-term return sensitivities with neural networks to reduce instability and sample demand.
evidence_signal: The approach is evaluated on two challenging parametric navigation problems in unsteady flows, where it outperforms state-of-the-art RL methods and scales to high-dimensional state/action spaces.
watch_item: Performance depends on access to differentiable environment models, so the method may be harder to apply when system dynamics are not accurately differentiable or are only partially known.
paper_title: Physics-enhanced reinforcement learning for real-time optimal control of dynamical systems
